title: corylus avellana var. pontica
intro: Corylus avellana var. pontica, also known as the Turkish hazelnut, is a variety of the hazelnut tree (Corylus avellana) native to the Caucasus region and Turkey. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Corylus avellana var. pontica include its ability to support cardiovascular health, lower cholesterol levels, and improve cognitive function, as well as its potential to protect against certain types of cancer. The herbal preparations of Corylus avellana var. pontica include infusions, teas, and supplements, which can be made from its leaves, bark, and nuts, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
